evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Endpoints used (discord.com/api/v10, /oauth2/token, /users/@me, /users/@me/guilds, /applications/{app_id}/guilds/{guild_id}/commands) and Ed25519 signature verification pattern via PyNaCl are internally consistent with documented Discord API patterns. Scopes identify+guilds and applications.commands correctly referenced. No version claim with date, but consistent throughout.
evidence.executability: Code is spread across many separate fenced blocks (config.py, main.py, auth.py, models.py, discord_commands.py, interactions.py) with cross-imports; extractor writing each to a file would mostly work, but main.py snippet imports register_guild_slash_commands from discord_commands which is only implied. No requirements.txt block, no /health endpoint defined though test_healthcheck assumes it. Multiple non-obvious fixes needed. Mentions ngrok for dev (sandbox infeasibility pattern) but only as discussion, not a required command.
evidence.completeness: (a) Credential acquisition described in prose (Developer Portal steps) but no explicit URLs; (b) install commands described in prose, no ```bash block with pip install; (c) env vars referenced in Settings class but no explicit .env template block; (d) backend code present in fragments; (e) frontend code present; (f) test snippets present but no single runnable test command. Multiple critical gaps: no bash install block, no .env template, no consolidated test command.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Citations are bare numeric refs [1][2][12] etc. with no URLs. No canonical discord.com/developers/docs URLs anchored near code. Cannot verify any official docs URL was cited.

rubric_notes: ngrok mentioned in prose as dev option, not as a required executable command, so EXECUTABILITY not auto-capped at 2. Major weakness is lack of bash install block, .env template, and absence of any URL citations despite extensive numeric reference style.
